extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon attacked with 4.5km remaining after GC group reformed post-KARAPAZ solo break, taking stage while KARAPAZ seized pink jersey.
tactical_patterns: Bora-Hansgrohe executed pre-planned race destruction with 80km remaining, using ALEOTI then KELDERMAN to eliminate breakaway and split peloton early.
KARAPAZ attacked at 28.6km on penultimate climb, opened 30 seconds solo, but elite GC chasers organized cooperation to reel him back.
NIBALI's counter-attack on final classified climb with HINDLEY was critical regrouping move that neutralized KARAPAZ's advantage before technical descent.
YATES exploited 20-minute GC deficit to attack inside 5km when others calculating time gaps, proving non-contenders dangerous on hard stages.

rider_signals.KARAPAZ Richard: positive: demonstrated race-winning aggression with 28km solo move and took pink despite being caught, showing Tour-winning form returning
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: controlled chase on final climb looking like his 2016 Giro-winning self, bridged to KARAPAZ efficiently with HINDLEY
rider_signals.LOPEZ Juanpe: negative: defended well initially but cracked when KARAPAZ attacked 28.6km out, dropped from 1st to 9th GC
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: positive: followed NIBALI's decisive chase move and took second, now only 7 seconds from pink with team executing perfectly
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: missed critical 65km-to-go split created by Bora's tempo, losing significant time before mountain even started
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: continuously lost time throughout stage despite being in initial front group, slipping badly on GC
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: positive: worked back from missed split to finish 39 seconds down, limiting losses to move into podium position
